preview_text:
The model backs Netherlands as clear favourite at 68%, leaving Sweden just 8% to win outright. That 60-percentage-point gap reflects a pronounced imbalance in the forecast; the underdog's chances rest almost entirely on a draw or an upset, neither of which the model rates as probable.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Netherlands favoured decisively, but a draw remains plausible at 24%. Sweden's path narrows considerably in this forecast—the model sees the match as competitive in structure (a draw is live) but tilted heavily toward a Netherlands victory rather than a genuinely close three-way contest.

The model and sportsbook diverge materially on Sweden's chances. Books strip to 20% for a Sweden win; the model halves that to 8%, a 12-percentage-point gap. Polymarket aligns closely with sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view of Sweden's upside is correct; the discrepancy is worth noting for users comparing statistical and market perspectives.
